message:
data(extensions): update conformance manifest, catalog, and candidate pool

Refresh extension ecosystem data files with latest scan and validation
results:

- VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json: update conformance validation status for all
  tested extensions with current pass/fail results and coverage metrics
- SHA256SUMS.txt: add integrity hashes for 9 newly archived npm extension
  artifacts (oh-my-pi suite, pi-interview, qualisero-scip, tmustier-arcade)
- extension-artifact-provenance.json: new provenance tracking for artifact
  download sources, versions, and timestamps
- extension-candidate-pool.json: expand candidate pool with ~4500 lines of
  new entries from npm registry scans and GitHub discovery
- extension-entry-scan.json: updated entry point scan results for all
  candidates
- extension-master-catalog.json: refresh master catalog with latest
  metadata and classification
- EXTENSION_POPULARITY_CRITERIA.md: minor criteria refinements for
  scoring methodology

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -296,15 +296,16 @@ cargo run --bin ext_popularity_snapshot -- \
 ```
 
 Notes:
-- GitHub metrics require `GITHUB_TOKEN` in the environment.
-- If `GITHUB_TOKEN` is missing, GitHub lookups are skipped and npm signals are still refreshed.
+- GitHub metrics use `GITHUB_TOKEN` when present, and otherwise fall back to `gh auth token` if
+  GitHub CLI authentication is available.
+- If neither token source is available, GitHub lookups are skipped and npm signals are still refreshed.
 - Use `--dry-run` to preview summary stats without writing files.
 
 Example run:
 
 ```bash
 cargo run --bin ext_score_candidates -- \
-  --input docs/extension-scoring-input.json \
+  --input docs/extension-candidate-pool.json \
   --out docs/extension-priority.json \
   --summary-out docs/extension-priority-summary.json \
   --as-of 2026-02-06T00:00:00Z \
